title: guichenotia micrantha
intro: Guichenotia micrantha, also known as the small-leaved Guichenotia, is a flowering plant species native to Western Australia. It is a shrub or small tree in the family Malvaceae, known for its small yellow flowers and edible leaves. Guichenotia micrantha has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The leaves of the plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, offering potential health benefits including reduced inflammation and improved respiratory function.
